raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I contacted Best Buy customer service via chat to request a price match for a recently purchased XXXX XXXX XXXX. The agent reviewed the competitor link, verified the SKU and eligibility, and confirmed in writing that the price match was approved. I was instructed to cancel my previous order and repurchase the correct item, and I did so using my Best Buy Credit Card issued by Citibank. 

The Best Buy agent then confirmed the refund amount of {$310.00} and provided a specific case ID ( XXXX ), stating the refund had been successfully submitted. I relied on this confirmation to complete the transaction in good faith. 

Several days later, Best Buy sent an email reversing the refund approval, stating that the item was no longer in stock from the competitor. However, the product was in stock at the time the agent reviewed and approved the price match, and I had already acted on that confirmation. The denial was issued after the refund was promised and after I had completed the purchase per Best Buys instruction. 

I filed a billing dispute with Citibank under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), providing the chat transcript, the order receipt, the refund denial email, and all necessary supporting documentation. Despite this, Citibank denied the dispute without providing any written explanation or justification. 

It appears that Citibank failed to conduct a reasonable investigation as required under 15 U.S.C. 1666. Given the clear evidence of misrepresentation and breach of agreement by the merchant, I believe Citibank may have inappropriately sided with its co-branded partner, Best Buy, rather than acting in good faith to protect me as the cardholder. I am seeking a reversal of the dispute decision and a permanent credit of {$310.00}.
